title: `podman build` appears to ignore `--arch` option when pulling multiarch images
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I'm trying to build a non-native container with `podman build --arch arm64 Dockerfile` on an armv7a userland.  I don't necessarily expect this to work.

I would, however, hope that `podman` would pull the `arm64` variant of the multiarch image referenced (by tag ("latest"), rather than by sha256 hash) in the `Dockerfile` file since the `--arch` option was given.  This may not actually be usable once fetched - but the override should, I believe, still apply since it was explicitly given.

(Re-reading the help text suggests that the value of `--arch` might only be used when adding parameters to a newly created image - in which case please consider this instead a Feature Request to either add an option to override the architecture pulled, or dual-purpose this existing option!)

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Pull a multiarch image on an `arm` host with the `--arch` argument to `podman build` set to `arm64`

2. Inspect the pulled image.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
"Architecture": "arm"
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```
"Architecture": "arm64"
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Git Commit:   a0d478edea7f775b7ce32f8eb1a01e75374486cb
Built:        Thu Dec 31 13:48:19 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/arm
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  arch: arm
  buildahVersion: 1.18.0

  conmon:
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
Their are hidden options
--override-arch and --override-variant that will allow you to move forward.  I am working on fixing this in buildah, now and will move to fixing it in Podman as soon as the buildah merges.

https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2868

Fixed in podman 3.0
```
$ arch
x86_64
$ ./bin/podman run --arch arm64 ubi8 arch
Resolved "ubi8" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob cbe902a0a8c4 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e753ad39f085 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 70dab2c4ec done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
aarch64
$ ./bin/podman build --arch arm64 /tmp/
STEP 1: FROM ubi8
STEP 2: RUN arch
aarch64
--> Using cache e6de2269f2fd6d4a7e9edb93aa6862c22e5d4ed3053445ed2e3e3a528fb30f6e
--> e6de2269f2f
e6de2269f2fd6d4a7e9edb93aa6862c22e5d4ed3053445ed2e3e3a528fb30f6e
```

I think this bug has arisen again

```
$ arch
i386
$ podman run --arch arm64 ubi8 arch
Resolved "ubi8" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:0e6d2c893225d645081f48c1a8e0bb1e355ab69b75e8398ca4983a428bc22e57
Copying blob sha256:3163c3561e0cdd9c8f739672c089c716904d62dcf5b45d36446d258a69256806
Copying config sha256:9c1f1dfba3aeabe0788d6e38ca997e37384be8afc55250804d14b31e1c07e464
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
{"msg":"exec container process `/usr/bin/arch`: Exec format error","level":"error","time":"2021-10-26T14:27:11.000723953Z"}
```

Below are the podman details

```
$ podman version
Client:
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.17.2
Built:        Wed Oct 20 02:44:42 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.0
API Version:  3.4.0
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Fri Oct  1 01:10:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
Do you have the equivalent of qemu-user-static installed?

I'm trying to install qemu-user-static but getting below error

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15676216/139018801-f9b225db-cbde-446d-972a-ad2ff1eaea75.png)

Tried to run as `root` user also

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15676216/139018992-e43fa361-b662-4c02-b013-819b23f92d1b.png)

I am not sure how that is supposed to work, I was suggesting that you do the equivalent of 
yum -y install qemu-user-static
on your host platform, not attempt to run it within a container.

I'm on MacOs and homebrew doesn't have this package yet. 

The above command of running `qemu-user-static` in a container does work with `docker` but getting error with `podman`

I think that is because the OS does not match.
I think you need to do 
podman machine ssh
Then do 
sudo yum -y install qemu-user-static
